fields.summary: Allow ResourceRequests to specify ExecutionType of a request ask
Extend YARN to support distributed scheduling

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.
status.name: Resolved

issuetype.description: A new feature of the product, which has yet to be developed.
issuetype.name: New Feature

fields.description:
YARN-2882 introduced container types that are internal (not user-facing) and are used by the ContainerManager during execution at the NM.

With this JIRA we are introducing (user-facing) resource request types that are used by the AM to specify the type of the ResourceRequest.

resolution.description: A fix for this issue is checked into the tree and tested.
resolution.name: Fixed

fields.summary: Fix ContainerRequest Constructor to set nodelabelExpression correctly

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.
status.name: Resolved

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.
status.name: Resolved

comments.body: Marking this as Duplicate since the YARN-2882 patch contains all the needed changes for adding the Execution type to the resource request
Reopening issue as we decided to not include the ExecutionType in the ResourceRequest for YARN-2882.
Attaching patch for this adding ExecutionType to ResourceRequest.

[~asuresh], [~kasha], I reopened this issue and attached a patch.
Please give it a look whenever you can.

My impression from the offline conversation was the AMs should not need to request a specific ExecutionType. Would like to understand this better: are there any prerequisites for a container to be executed opportunistically? Are there applications that favor guaranteed vs opportunistic execution, and is that bias justified? 

In any case, let us drive YARN-2882 to resolution while we figure these things out.

Thanks [~kkaranasos],

The patch generally looks good to me, some nits:
- Javadocs of getExecutionType is wrong.
- I would prefer to keep new APIs to be unstable so we could update them before features become stable
- Javadocs of ExecutionType: if we use ExecutionType for resource request, I would suggest to add description that scheduler could use it to decide if idle resources could be used by the resource request.

Thank you for the feedback, [~leftnoteasy].

I addressed your first two comments and will now upload a new version of the patch.

bq. Javadocs of ExecutionType: if we use ExecutionType for resource request, I would suggest to add description that scheduler could use it to decide if idle resources could be used by the resource request.
Just double-checking: do you mean adding an additional description in the javadoc of the ExecutionType?
Let me know and I will go ahead and update the patch.

New version of the patch, addressing [~leftnoteasy]'s comments.
# Fixed getExeceutionType javadoc.
# Marked new APIs in ResourceRequest as unstable.

Thanks [~kkaranasos] for updating the patch

bq. Just double-checking: do you mean adding an additional description in the javadoc of the ExecutionType?
Yes

And:
{code}
87	  @Public
88	  @Stable
89	  public static ResourceRequest newInstance(Priority priority, String hostName, ...
{code}
Should be unstable as well.

As [~kasha] [commented|https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/YARN-1011?focusedCommentId=15076749&page=com.atlassian.jira.plugin.system.issuetabpanels:comment-tabpanel#comment-15076749] on YARN-1011:
bq. Given this complication, I would prefer we do not involve AMs in the decision-making process. Based on the need and usecases, we could revisit this at a later time. Note that YARN-4335 adds this to ResourceRequest for distributed scheduling, and even there they are not entirely sure if it needs to be a part.

I'm not sure if there's any tasks of YARN-2877 directly depends on it, It's fine to me to commit this to YARN-2877 branch, but I prefer to hold commit unless any tasks require it.
[~leftnoteasy], YARN-2885 depends on this.. and YARN-4412 depends on YARN-2885

Updating patch:
# Turned one of the new APIs from stable to unstable
# Additional information in the javadoc of the ExecutionType

[~leftnoteasy], thanks again for the feedback.
I addressed your latest comments in my last patch -- please give it a look and let me know if it looks OK, so we can go ahead and push it to the branch.
+1 to latest patch, thanks [~kkaranasos]!
Thanks [~leftnoteasy] for the review and [~kkaranasos] for the patch. I will commit to yarn-2877 branch after jenkins.
